$comment: Project-scoped Claude Code settings. The version-controlled architecture surface (rules/skills/agents/hooks here, settings.json itself) — per-machine state lives in .claude/settings.local.json (gitignored). Hooks wire the .claude/hooks/*.sh enforcement scripts. Worktree config locks in the parallel-session workflow. Authoritative external: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide + https://code.claude.com/docs/en/worktrees + https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents.
$comment_baseRef: worktree.baseRef='fresh' (2026-06-04) — flipped from 'head' per docs/audits/2026-06-03-claude-code-workflow-controls.md §13 #4 + PR #458 incident: an EnterWorktree call inherited the operator's deferred-arc checkout instead of origin/main, producing a worktree branched from stale state. 'fresh' makes every EnterWorktree (and every subagent with `isolation: worktree`) branch from origin/main by default. The CI sentinel `.github/workflows/branch-base-sentinel.yml` is the PR-time backstop. If you ever need to stack a PR on another in-flight branch, override per-call (pass an explicit base ref to `git worktree add -b … <base>`) rather than flipping this default back.
$comment_permissions: permissions.deny (2026-06-04, controls-audit #5) — Anthropic-canonical second layer of defense, enforced by Claude Code itself (not by hooks; hooks can be bypassed with env-var overrides — deny rules cannot). Per https://code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions: rules evaluate deny → ask → allow, first match wins, scoped denies leave the tool available but block matching calls. Three classes: (1) secret files in this project tree + credential dirs in $HOME (.env*, secrets/**, ~/.ssh/**, ~/.aws/**, ~/.gnupg/**, ~/.netrc, ~/.config/gh/**) — Claude can still RUN gh + git because subprocesses read their own configs; the deny only blocks Claude's Read tool. (2) Network calls Claude shouldn't make directly — STE uses Python httpx/requests for HTTP; curl/wget have zero legit usage in tpcore/ops/scripts (verified 2026-06-04) and are a common prompt-injection exfil vector. (3) Destructive ops that should NEVER happen from a Claude session: root/home rm -rf, dd if=, chmod -R 777, chown -R. Sentinel: tests/test_permissions_deny_present.py.
